planMap.description: Resolved plan mapping by key (includes existing free plan)

checkoutUrl.description: Full checkout URL based on backend configuration (ready to redirect customer)

customerUrl.description: Full customer URL based on backend configuration (ready to redirect customer)

externalRef.description: External reference ID from your auth system (if set during creation or update)

customerUrl.description: Full customer URL based on backend configuration (ready to redirect customer)

remainingUnits.description: Estimated whole units remaining from prepaid credit balance

price.description: Price in smallest currency unit (e.g. cents)

activationRequired.description: True when the customer must activate a priced default plan before usage is allowed

balance.description: Prepaid usage balance context when the default plan is usage-based

checkoutSessionId.description: Checkout session ID if payment is required

checkoutUrl.description: Checkout URL if payment is required

confirmationUrl.description: Customer portal confirmation URL when activation is required (fallback when not starting checkout)

creditBalance.description: Credit balance in mils (for pre-paid usage-based plans)

creditsPerUnit.description: Credits per usage unit (for pre-paid usage-based plans)

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code for credit fields

meterName.description: The meter name to use when tracking usage events

plans.description: Active plans on the product available for activation or checkout

product.description: Product the limit check applies to

remaining.description: Remaining usage units before hitting the limit

withinLimits.description: Whether the customer is within their usage limits

mcpServer.description: Created or updated MCP server identity

planMap.description: Resolved plan mapping by bootstrap key

toolsAutoMapped.description: True when tools were auto-discovered from origin because the request omitted tools

isActive.description: Whether the plan is active (derived from status)

measures.description: What the plan measures for usage tracking

rolloverUnusedUnits.description: Whether unused units roll over to next period

amount.description: Amount in USD cents (normalised for aggregation)

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code of the customer-facing charge

exchangeRate.description: Exchange rate from original currency to USD

originalAmount.description: Original amount in the payment currency (minor units)

planRef.description: Plan reference from the plan snapshot, for reliable plan matching

planSnapshot.description: Snapshot of the plan at time of purchase

country.description: ISO-3166 alpha-2 country code of the merchant

iconUrl.description: Absolute URL to the square app icon / logomark. Consumed by MCP host chromes, mobile avatar slots, and any surface where the landscape `logoUrl` would need letterboxing.

legalName.description: Legal entity name used in SCA mandate copy

logoUrl.description: Absolute URL to the merchant logo

statementDescriptor.description: Descriptor appearing on the customer card statement

measures.description: What the plan measures for usage tracking

rolloverUnusedUnits.description: Whether unused units roll over to next period

name.description: Plan name captured at purchase time

stripePublishableKey.description: SolvaPay's platform Stripe publishable key for the authenticated provider's environment. Safe to expose browser-side; paired with the connected `accountId` returned from `create-payment-intent` for Stripe Connect direct charges. Omitted when not configured so callers can fall back cleanly to a hosted flow.

isMcpPay.description: Whether this product uses MCP Pay proxy

mcp.description: MCP linkage details for MCP-enabled products

amount.description: Amount in USD cents (normalised for aggregation)

exchangeRate.description: Exchange rate from original currency to USD

metadata.description: Arbitrary metadata attached to the purchase

originalAmount.description: Original amount in the payment currency (cents/pence)

planSnapshot.description: Plan snapshot at time of purchase (null for credit topups)

usage.description: Usage billing state for usage-based plans

price.description: Price in minor currency units (e.g. cents)

status.example: Active subscription: My API Product (25% usage consumed)

post.description: Creates a hosted checkout session for a customer to purchase a product plan. Returns a session ID and checkout URL for redirect.

get.description: Retrieves a customer's details using their unique reference ID, externalRef, or email. Use query parameter 'reference' to look up by customer reference, 'externalRef' for external auth ID, or 'email' for customer email. Exactly one parameter must be provided. Returns the customer's name, email, and active purchases. Only customers owned by the authenticated provider can be accessed.

parameters.description: Customer reference identifier (use exactly one query parameter)
parameters.name: reference

parameters.description: External reference ID from your auth system (use exactly one query parameter)
parameters.name: externalRef

parameters.description: Customer email address (use exactly one query parameter)
parameters.name: email

example.message: Must provide exactly one of reference, externalRef, or email

400.description: Invalid request - must provide exactly one of reference, externalRef, or email

get.summary: Get customer by reference, externalRef, or email

post.description: Creates a new customer record for the authenticated provider. Customers represent end-users who will purchase your products. Email is required and must be unique per provider. The name field is optional but recommended for better tracking.

400.description: Invalid email or missing required fields

get.description: Retrieves a customer's details using their unique reference ID. Returns the customer's name, email, and active purchases. Only customers owned by the authenticated provider can be accessed.

example.message: Customer with reference cus_3c4d5e6f7g8h not found

patch.description: Updates an existing customer identified by reference. Use this to backfill or change fields such as `externalRef`, `name`, or `email`. Only the fields supplied in the body are modified.

get.description: Returns the credit balance for a customer identified by reference.

example.message: Customer with reference cus_3c4d5e6f7g8h not found

post.description: Creates a customer session URL that can be used to redirect customers to the customer page. Returns the customer URL and session ID. The session is short-lived (15 minutes) for security reasons.

400.description: Invalid request data or customer not found

get.description: Retrieves a customer session by its sessionId with all data hydrated including customer details and purchases. The session must belong to the authenticated provider.

post.description: Checks whether a customer has an active purchase for a product and is within their usage limits. Returns a checkout URL if payment is required.
post.summary: Check usage limits for a customer and product

get.description: Returns the subset of provider data safe to render in a customer-facing checkout: display name, legal name, support contact, terms and privacy URLs, and logo. Used by SDK components like MandateText and CheckoutSummary.
get.summary: Get merchant identity for the authenticated provider

post.description: Records a single usage event against a named meter.

400.description: Invalid meter name or meter is archived

post.description: Persists each event with recordUsage (same shape as single POST), after shared meter/customer checks.

400.description: Invalid meter name or meter is archived

get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of all payment intents created by the authenticated provider. Supports pagination through limit and offset query parameters.
get.summary: List payment intents for the provider

post.description: Creates a new payment intent for a customer to purchase a plan. Requires an idempotency key to prevent duplicate charges. Returns client secret and publishable key needed for frontend integration.

parameters.description: Unique idempotency key to prevent duplicate payments (required)
parameters.name: idempotency-key

400.description: Missing required fields or invalid data

post.description: Processes a payment intent that has been confirmed on the client side. Polls the database for payment intent status to become succeeded (up to 10 seconds). Returns the current status of the payment intent.

parameters.description: Payment processor ID returned from createPaymentIntent
parameters.name: processorPaymentId

timeout.description: Timeout waiting for payment intent confirmation

value.message: Timeout while waiting for payment intent confirmation, try again later. This could be due to payment webhooks not being configured correctly.

message.description: Optional message, only present for timeout status
message.example: Timeout while waiting for payment intent confirmation, try again later. This could be due to payment webhooks not being configured correctly.

400.description: Payment not succeeded, invalid request, or forbidden

post.summary: Process payment intent after client-side confirmation

get.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific payment intent including amount, currency, and client secret.

parameters.description: Payment intent reference or processor payment ID
parameters.name: reference

get.description: Returns the customer's default card for the authenticated provider. Sourced from stored payment-method records (no Stripe round-trip). Returns `{ kind: 'none' }` when no card is on file.

200.description: The customer's default card, or `{ kind: 'none' }` when no card is on file.

get.summary: Get the default payment method for a customer

get.description: Returns browser-safe platform values resolved against the provider environment. Today: the SolvaPay platform Stripe publishable key. This endpoint is the canonical home for future platform-wide, environment-gated SDK config (API version hints, feature flags, public hosted URLs) — additions land here instead of bloating /sdk/merchant (strictly provider identity) or /sdk/payment-intents (runs too late for pre-intent UI decisions).
get.summary: Get environment-aware platform config for the authenticated provider

get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of products for the authenticated provider. Supports filtering by status, search term, and MCP Pay flag.

post.description: Creates a new product for the authenticated provider.

400.description: Missing required fields or validation error

delete.description: Deletes a product permanently in sandbox. In live mode, soft-deletes (preserves data but hides from listings). If the product has purchases in live mode, deactivates instead.

get.description: Retrieves a product by its reference or ID, including embedded plans.

put.description: Updates an existing product. Only provided fields are updated.

post.description: Deep-copies a product including all its plans. Generates new references for the clone.

parameters.description: Product reference or ID to clone
parameters.name: productRef

put.description: Configures plans and optional tool-to-plan mappings for an MCP product.

400.description: Invalid MCP plans request or product is not MCP-enabled

put.summary: Configure MCP plans on an MCP product

total.description: Total number of plans for the product

post.summary: Create a plan for a product

delete.summary: Delete a plan from a product

get.summary: Get a plan for a product

put.summary: Update a plan for a product

post.description: Creates an MCP-enabled product with a unified plans array, configures origin URL, and maps tools to plans in one request.

get.description: Retrieves all purchases for the authenticated provider with optional filtering by status, product, or customer.
Retrieves a single purchase including plan snapshot, usage, and billing details.

post.description: Cancels an active purchase. For recurring purchases, cancellation takes effect at the end of the current billing period.
Reactivates a purchase that was cancelled but has not yet reached its end date. Restores auto-renewal and clears cancellation fields.

get.description: Retrieves all purchases for a specific customer. Useful for billing history and access checks.
Retrieves all purchases for a specific product. Each billing period creates a new purchase document.

post.description: Records a usage event into the Usage timeseries collection.
Same persistence rules as POST /sdk/usages for each item: validate batch first, then one insert per event.
Returns customer profile, active purchase details including usage and plan info, and a customer portal URL.
post.summary: Get user info and purchase status
